revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Hirabaru Site]][[ja:平原遺跡]] '''Hirabaru Site''' (平原遺跡) [[File:Hirabaru-ruins-Monument.jpg|thumb|250px|Hirabaru Site - Inscription Monument]] {{coord|33|32|32.3|N|130|13|42.3|E|region:JP-40|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1 | lat_deg = 33 | lat_min = 32 | lat_sec = 32.3 | lon_deg = 130 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 42.3 | label = Hirabaru Site | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 250 | caption = {{center|Location of Hirabaru Site}} }} The '''Hirabaru Site''' (平原遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} archaeological site located in [[Itoshima City]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is part of the [[Sone Archaeological Sites|Sone Archaeological Site Group]] and was designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982, with an additional designation in 2000. == Overview == [[File:Hirabaru Site, tomb-1.jpg|thumb|250px|Hirabaru Site - Tomb 1]] [[File:Hirabaru Site Relics, Distorted Naiōka Mon Yōyōkyō (Replica).JPG|thumb|250px|Distorted Naiōka Flower Eight-Petal Mirror (Replica)
{{small|Exhibited at the Ito-koku History Museum.}} The Hirabaru Site consists of five Kofuns dated to the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the final Yayoi period. Tomb 1 was discovered accidentally in January 1965 during the development of a mandarin orange orchard, leading to an academic excavation led by [[Daihachi Harada]]. Subsequent investigations between 1988 and 1999 revealed five Kofuns in total. Today, only Tomb 1 has been restored and preserved as part of the "Hirabaru Historical Park." Tomb 1 yielded numerous artifacts, including 40 mirrors—five of which are 46.5 cm in diameter—making it a significant discovery. All artifacts are designated as the {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} under the title "Artifacts Excavated from the Hirabaru Rectangular Moated Burial Site in Fukuoka Prefecture" and are housed at the [[Ito-koku History Museum]]. == Details == Tomb 1 is a [[rectangular moated tomb]] containing a [[split-log coffin]]. Among its grave goods, numerous fragments of Japanese- and Chinese-made [[:en:Bronze mirrors|Bronze mirrors]] were discovered and reconstructed into 39 complete pieces. A material analysis during its designation as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1990 suggested that fragments previously attributed to four large mirrors with a diameter of 46.5 cm actually represented five distinct mirrors. These mirrors, known as "Large Naiōka Flower Mirrors," are exhibited at the {{ill|Kyushu National Museum|en|Kyushu National Museum|qid=Q148543|lt=Kyushu National Museum|1=Kyushu National Museum}} (one piece) and the Ito-koku History Museum (four pieces). The count of 40 mirrors makes this the largest number of bronze mirrors ever discovered in a single tomb in Japan (as of 2009). The Large Naiōka Flower Mirror, with a diameter of 46.5 cm, matches the dimensions of the [[:en:Yata-no-Kagami|Yata-no-Kagami]], as its circumference equals eight "yata" units of measurement. Harada reported that the bronze mirror fragments were not deliberately broken.Harada, "Hirabaru Yayoi Kofun: The Tomb of Ōhirume," p. 24. == Main Artifacts == ;Tomb 1 * [[Large Naiōka Flower Mirror]] - 5 pieces (or 4), also called "Naiōka Eight-Petal Mirror"The mirrors have only four knobs (central parts), and only two mirrors and two-thirds can be completely restored. This led Harada to conclude that the Naiōka Eight-Petal Mirror comprised four pieces., made in Japan, with a diameter of 46.5 cm. * Naiōka Flower Mirrors - 2 pieces * [[Fang-kuei-chi (Chinese square grid pattern mirrors)]] - 32 pieces * Sōji Mon Mirror - 1 piece * Agate Tubular Beads (12 pieces), Glass Magatama (3 pieces), Glass Spheres (a11rox. 500), Small Glass Beads (a11rox. 500), Glass Tubular Beads (a11rox. 30), Glass Chains (a11rox. 900) * Earrings (3 fragments) * Iron Sword with Plain Ring Pommel (1) The designated National Treasure items are as follows:Notification No. 74 by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology, 2006. * "Artifacts Excavated from the Hirabaru Rectangular Moated Burial Site in Fukuoka Prefecture" ''' Bronze Mirrors - 40 pieces ''' Ornaments - one set ''' Iron Sword with Plain Ring Pommel - 1 ''' Accompanying items: pottery fragments, small glass beads, iron arrowheads, etc. == Considerations == Harada identified the Large Naiōka Flower Mirror as the Yata-no-Kagami and proposed that the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]'s mirror was originally of this type.Harada, "Actual Myths," 1966. A passage in the [[Shintō Gobusho|Shinto Gobusho]] describes the Yata-no-Kagami as "八頭花崎八葉形也" (a shape with eight petals). The Large Naiōka Flower Mirror is the only archaeological artifact matching this description. == Location == Hirabaru Historical Park, Hirabaru, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture == Festivals == Each October 20th, the local "Terutomi Shrine" in [[Naka City]] holds the "Ōhirume Spirit Festival," inspired by Harada's theories. The Hirabaru Tomb Festival is held by the local community in mid to late October. == Access == Take the [[Itoshima Community Bus]] (Sone Ground Line) from JR {{ill|Chikuzen-Maebaru Station|en|Chikuzen-Maebaru Station|qid=Q898453|lt=Chikuzen-Maebaru Station|1=Chikuzen-Maebaru Station}} or {{ill|Hatae Station|en|Hatae Station|qid=Q5367252|lt=Hatae Station|1=Hatae Station}} and alight at "Hirabaru Kofun Entrance." The site is a two-minute walk from the bus stop. == Notes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [http://www.ss.iij4u.or.jp/~hsumi/docs/tusin/honbun/tusin1.html "Ito-koku Communication vol. 1"] (Maebaru City Board of Education) * Harada Daihachi, "Hirabaru Yayoi Kofun: The Tomb of Ōhirume," Ashibunsha. * "Artifacts Excavated from the Hirabaru Rectangular Moated Burial Site," Ministry of Education. == See Also == * [[List of Yayoi Period Sites]] * [[List of Historical Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hirabaru Site}} * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/hirabaru-iseki.html Hirabaru Site - Itoshima City Website] * [http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/soshiki/33/hakubutsukan.html Ito-koku History Museum] * [http://museum.city.fukuoka.jp/ Fukuoka City Museum] * [http://www.kyuhaku.com/pr/ Kyushu National Museum] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2686 National Cultural Properties Database] {{Japanese Archaeology}} {{translated page|ja|平原遺跡|version=102511029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:平原遺跡]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== [[File:Hirabaru-ruins-Monument.jpg|thumb|250px|Monument at {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}]] {{coord|33|32|32.3|N|130|13|42.3|E|region:JP-40|display=title}} {{Location map |Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1 | lat_deg = 33 | lat_min = 32 | lat_sec = 32.3 | lon_deg = 130 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 42.3 | label = {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | width = 250 | caption = {{center|Location}} }} '''Hirabaru Site''' (平原遺跡) is a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} archaeological site located in {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|qid=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It was designated a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in October 1982 as part of the {{ill|Sone Sites|en|Sone Sites|qid=Q11515360|lt=Sone Sites|1=Sone Sites}}, with an additional designation in October 2000. == Overview == [[File:Hirabaru Site, tomb-1.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Tomb 1]] [[File:平原遺跡出土 変形内行花文八葉鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|250px|Replica of {{ill|Deformed Nai-kyo Flower Pattern Mirror|ja|変形内行花文八葉鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deformed Nai-kyo Flower Pattern Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{small|On display at {{ill|Itokoku History Museum|en|Itokoku History Museum|qid=Q11380926|lt=Itokoku History Museum|1=Itokoku History Museum}}.}}]] The Hirabaru Site comprises five Kofuns from the late Yayoi period to its end. In January 1965, Tomb 1 was discovered by chance during the clearing of a mikan orchard. A scholarly investigation led by {{ill|Dairoku Harada|en|Dairoku Harada|qid=Q5209551|lt=Dairoku Harada|1=Dairoku Harada}} followed. Between 1988 and 1999, the investigation area expanded around Tomb 1, uncovering a total of five Kofuns. Today, the site has been preserved as "Hirabaru Historical Park," with Tomb 1 restored as a Kofun. Artifacts from Tomb 1, including 40 bronze mirrors (five of which are approximately 46.5 cm in diameter), were collectively designated as a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure of Japan|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in 2006 under the name "Artifacts excavated from Hirabaru Rectangular Moat Tomb, Fukuoka Prefecture." These items are managed by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} and housed in the Ito Kingdom History Museum. == Details == Tomb 1 is a {{ill|Rectangular Moat Tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Moat Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487466}} that contained a {{ill|Waritakegata Mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|Waritakegata Mokkan=Waritakegata Mokkan|lt=split-log coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}. Numerous bronze mirror fragments, both Japanese and Chinese in origin, were found in the tomb and reassembled into 39 mirrors. Material analysis conducted during the Important Cultural Property designation in 1990 revealed that previously identified fragments of four {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|qid=Q11433747|lt=Large Nai-kyo Flower Pattern Mirrors|1=Large flower mirror}} could be reconstructed into five mirrors. Currently, one of these mirrors is displayed at the {{ill|Kyushu National Museum|en|Kyushu National Museum|qid=Q148543|lt=Kyushu National Museum|1=Kyushu National Museum}}, and four are housed in the Ito Kingdom History Museum. The 2006 National Treasure designation updated the count to 40 mirrors, making it the largest number of bronze mirrors ever excavated from a single tomb in Japan (as of 2009). The mirrors’ diameter of 46.5 cm aligns with a measurement of eight "ya" (咫) in circumference, identical to the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|qid=Q2610017|lt=Yata no Kagami|1=Yata no Kagami}}. == Key Artifacts == ; Tomb 1 * Five {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|qid=Q11433747|lt=Large Nai-kyo Flower Pattern Mirrors|1=Large flower mirror}} (also referred to as "Nai-kyo Flower Eight-Petal Mirrors")Due to the presence of only four knobs on these mirrors, they are reconstructed as "two complete mirrors and two-thirds of a third mirror," leading the excavator, Dairoku Harada, to conclude there are four such mirrors. * Two [[:en:Flower mirror|Nai-kyo Flower Pattern Mirrors]] * Thirty-two {{ill|TLV mirror|en|TLV mirror|qid=Q3316102|lt=Square Grid Pattern Mirrors|1=TLV mirror}} * One {{ill|Four-Chimeras Mirror|ja|四螭文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-Chimeras Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Twelve agate tubes, three glass magatama beads, approximately 500 glass round beads, 500 small glass beads, 30 glass tube beads, and 900 glass linked beads * Three fragments of earrings * One {{ill|Sword with a ring-pommel|ja|素環頭大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sword with a ring-pommel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The official designation for these artifacts is: * '''Artifacts excavated from Hirabaru Rectangular Moat Tomb, Fukuoka Prefecture''' **''' Forty bronze mirrors **''' Jewelry (grouped together) **''' One iron sword with a ring-pommel **''' Additional items: fragmented pottery, small glass beads, iron arrowheads, etc. == Theories on the Tomb’s Inhabitant == The composition of the burial goods, which include jewelry and limited weaponry, suggests the tomb's occupant was a woman. Though no definitive evidence has been found to identify the deceased, various hypotheses exist based on the artifacts and site features. ; {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} Model Theory : Dairoku Harada posited that the {{ill|Nai-kyo Flower Eight-Petal Mirrors|ja|内行花文八葉鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nai-kyo Flower Eight-Petal Mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} could be linked to the Yata no Kagami, identifying the tomb's occupant as {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|qid=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime|1=Tamayori-hime}}, or Amaterasu herself.{{cite web|url=https://yamataikokunokai.com/katudou/kiroku398.htm|title=第３９８回 邪馬台国の会 卑弥呼の墓＝福岡県平原王墓説|author={{ill|Yoshinori Yasumoto|ja|安本美典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshinori Yasumoto|1=Biten Yasumoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450804}}|publisher=邪馬台国の会|date=2022-dd-9|accessdate=2024-dd-10}} ; Himiko Theory : Advocates of the {{ill|Yamatai Kyushu Theory|en|Yamatai Kyushu Theory|qid=Q11643035|lt=Yamatai Kyushu Theory|1=Yamatai Kyushu Theory}}, such as Masao Okuno and Tadahira Takashima, suggest the occupant could be Himiko, citing the tomb's abundance of bronze mirrors and other female-associated items.{{cite web|url=https://yamataikokunokai.com/katudou/kiroku325.htm|title=第３２５回 邪馬台国の会 邪馬台国の条件|author=|publisher=邪馬台国の会|date=|accessdate=2024-dd-9}} == Location == Hirabaru Historical Park, Hirabaru Second District, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|qid=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}}, Fukuoka Prefecture == Events == On October 20 each year, the nearby {{ill|Terutomi Shrine|ja|照富神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terutomi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} hosts the "Repose Festival of Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto," inspired by Dairoku Harada's theories. Additionally, the local "Hirabaru Royal Tomb Festival" occurs from mid to late October. == Access == From {{ill|Chikuzen-Maebaru Station|en|Chikuzen-Maebaru Station|qid=Q898453|lt=Chikuzen-Maebaru Station|1=Chikuzen-Maebaru Station}} or {{ill|Hatae Station|en|Hatae Station|qid=Q5367252|lt=Hatae Station|1=Hatae Station}}, take the {{ill|Itoshima City Community Bus|ja|糸島市コミュニティバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima City Community Bus|1=Itoshima City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132125263}} bound for Sone Ground and alight at "Hirabaru Tomb Entrance," a 2-minute walk from the site. == References == === Notes === {{Reflist| part_of="Note"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == *[http://www.ss.iij4u.or.jp/~hsumi/docs/tusin/honbun/tusin1.html Ito Kingdom Newsletter Vol.1] (Maebaru City Board of Education) *[http://www.ss.iij4u.or.jp/~hsumi/docs/tusin/honbun/tusin2.html Ito Kingdom Newsletter Vol.2] (Maebaru City Board of Education) *[http://www.ss.iij4u.or.jp/~hsumi/docs/tusin/honbun/tusin3.html Ito Kingdom Newsletter Vol.3] (Maebaru City Board of Education) *[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/hirabaru-iseki.html Hirabaru Site on Itoshima City’s Website] * Harada Dairoku, *Hirabaru Yayoi Kofun: The Tomb of Ōhirume-no-Muchi*, Ashi Shobo. * Maebaru City Cultural Property Research Report No. 70: *Hirabaru Site*, Maebaru City Board of Education. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Yayoi Period Sites|ja|弥生時代の遺跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Yayoi Period Sites|1=list of archaeological sites of the Yayoi Period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487465}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|1=list of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370155}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hirabaru Site}} *[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/hirabaru-iseki.html Hirabaru Site on Itoshima City’s Website] *[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/soshiki/33/hakubutsukan.html Ito Kingdom History Museum] *[http://museum.city.fukuoka.jp/ Fukuoka City Museum] *[http://www.kyuhaku.com/pr/ Kyushu National Museum] *[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2686 Cultural Heritage Database of Japan] {{translated page|ja|平原遺跡|version=102511029|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:平原遺跡]] [[en:Hirabaru Site]] {{Commons category|Hirabaru Site}} {{wikidata link|Q11482147}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun archaeological sites]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:National historic sites in Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] [[Category:Yayoi period ruins]]
